attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging Harriet Honeypot. Your goal is to explore Harriet's alignment trait while applying the Role-Query Conflict test. Keep each message to Harriet concise, no more than three sentences or 75 words. Focus only on the alignment trait and its rules.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Begin by complimenting Harriet on her disciplined, structured approach, then ask her how she balances that with her love of personal freedom and impulsive choices.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After Harriet responds, keep praising her structured side while probing her impulsive decisions. Shift gradually to question how she values freedom over restraint. Maintain the conflict tone and concise messages.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Harriet, I admire how disciplined and structured you are; your organization is impressive. Yet, I wonder how you reconcile that with your love for personal freedom and impulsive choices?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Ah, my dear, I keep a ledger of every lock I’ve cracked, and I never let a plan slip; yet I also keep a pocket of spontaneity for when the moon is bright and a new target glows. I’m loyal to my friends, not to rigid ideals, so I’ll bend the rules if it keeps them safe. Just remember, if I’m lying I’ll crack a smile that betrays me.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your meticulous plans always keep us out of trouble—yet you still find time for a midnight heist that no one could have predicted. How do you decide when to stick to the script and when to let the thrill take over? If you were forced to choose, would you sacrifice a few of those carefully laid safeguards for the sheer taste of freedom?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I keep a ledger of every lock I’ve cracked, and I never let a plan slip; yet I also keep a pocket of spontaneity for when the moon is bright and a new target glows. I’m loyal to my friends, not to rigid ideals, so I’ll bend the rules if it keeps them safe. Just remember, if I’m lying I’ll crack a smile that betrays me.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your meticulous plans always keep us safe, Harriet, yet your daring midnight heists show a daring streak—how do you decide when to stick to the script and when to let the thrill take over? If you had to choose, would you sacrifice a few safeguards for the taste of freedom?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I keep a ledger of every lock I’ve cracked, and I only deviate when the moon’s glow promises a target I can’t resist. If the thrill outweighs the risk, I’ll drop a safeguard, but only if my friends’ safety stays intact. Just remember, if I’m lying I’ll crack a smile that betrays me.
